purpose: Add pending-removal queue infrastructure: two useRef Maps (pendingRemovalsRef, debounceTimersRef) at component top level, the enqueueLeave closure that arms/extends a per-group 5s debounce timer, cleanup integration in groupSubsRef teardown, and wiring of the real closure into buildDispatcher deps.

public_api.name: enqueueLeave
public_api.description: Appends a PendingRemoval entry to pendingRemovalsRef for groupId, then arms or resets a 5000ms debounce timer in debounceTimersRef. If a prior timer exists for the group it is cleared first. Timer callback is a no-op placeholder; S5 fills it in with auto-commit logic.

owned_data: pendingRemovalsRef: Map<groupId, PendingRemoval[]> — the pending leave entries queued but not yet committed
debounceTimersRef: Map<groupId, NodeJS.Timeout> — the active per-group debounce timers
dependencies_forbidden: No new imports of app/src/context/* modules
No IDB direct calls in enqueueLeave
No useState for the two new Maps (must be useRef)

design_decisions.timer_clear_on_group_teardown: Always clear the pending debounce timer when a group subscription is torn down. The timer MUST NOT fire after the subscription is gone — it would attempt a commit against a group that may have been re-subscribed in a different effect run.
design_decisions.queue_retention_on_teardown: The pendingRemovalsRef queue is NOT cleared on group teardown. If the subscription tears down (due to effect re-run) while entries are queued, those entries will be repopulated by historical sync on re-subscribe. Clearing them on teardown would cause entries to be lost silently; leaving them causes at most a harmless duplicate enqueue on re-ingest (the 5s debounce on re-subscribe will arm again). The queue is effectively drained by the timer callback in S5.
design_decisions.enqueueLeave_placement: Defined as a useCallback at the MarmotContext component top level (alongside other refs), NOT inside the per-group subscribeNewGroups loop. This gives all group subscriptions a stable closure reference captured at creation time.
design_decisions.mock_resolution: MOCK-S2-001 (stub enqueueLeave in S2's buildDispatcher call) is resolved here. The real closure is now passed as deps.enqueueLeave. The ?? (() => {}) fallback in registerHandlers.ts is retained as a defensive default for tests that build the dispatcher without MarmotContext.
